Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Computer-generated dream world: Virtual reality for a 286 processor 61 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Show HN: Timber – Ollama for classical ML models, 336x faster than Python 101 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. Decision trees – the unreasonable power of nested decision rules 460 upvotes, 74 comments
  4. Microgpt explained interactively 251 upvotes, 37 comments
  5. C64 Copy Protection 40 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Everett shuts down Flock camera network after judge rules footage public record 158 upvotes, 28 comments
  7. Right-sizes LLM models to your system's RAM, CPU, and GPU 94 upvotes, 22 comments
  8. Next-gen spacecraft are overwhelming communication networks 58 upvotes, 16 comments
  9. Show HN: Vibe Code your 3D Models 48 upvotes, 13 comments
  10. Ghostty – Terminal Emulator 694 upvotes, 302 comments
  11. Running Neural Amp Modeler on embedded hardware 24 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Tove Jansson's criticized illustrations of The Hobbit (2023) 142 upvotes, 64 comments
  13. Enable CORS for Your Blog 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  14. Little Free Library 103 upvotes, 48 comments
  15. You don't have to 79 upvotes, 35 comments
  16. WebMCP is available for early preview 253 upvotes, 137 comments
  17. How to record and retrieve anything you've ever had to look up twice 21 upvotes, 6 comments
  18. When does MCP make sense vs CLI? 355 upvotes, 226 comments
  19. Show HN: I built a zero-browser, pure-JS typesetting engine for bit-perfect PDFs 56 upvotes, 29 comments
  20. Evolving descriptive text of mental content from human brain activity 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  21. Ape Coding [fiction] 164 upvotes, 109 comments
  22. Why does C have the best file API 101 upvotes, 67 comments
  23. Have your cake and decompress it too 9 upvotes, 2 comments
  24. The inner workings of TCP zero-copy 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Process-Based Concurrency: Why Beam and OTP Keep Being Right 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  26. Frankensqlite a Rust reimplementation of SQLite with concurrent writers 38 upvotes, 32 comments
  27. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 151 upvotes, 169 comments
  28. Setting up phones is a nightmare 138 upvotes, 175 comments
  29. Long Range E-Bike (2021) 146 upvotes, 220 comments
  30. An interactive intro to Elliptic Curve Cryptography 3 upvotes, 1 comments
